title: Repayment Capacity Test
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you calculate your EMI capacity before taking a loan?
What factors should influence your EMI affordability decisions?

options.label: EMI below 20-30% of income
options.reflection: Correct! EMIs below 20-30% of income are generally considered safe and manageable.

options.label: EMI above 50% of income
options.reflection: EMI above 50% of income is very risky and leaves little room for other expenses.

options.label: Both are equally safe
options.reflection: EMI percentages vary greatly in risk level - higher percentages are more risky.

options.label: Income percentage doesn't matter for EMIs
options.reflection: Income percentage is crucial for determining EMI affordability and financial safety.

stages.prompt: What percentage of income is considered safe for EMI payments?

options.label: Between 40-50% of income
options.reflection: EMIs in this range are quite risky and leave little room for other expenses.

options.label: More than 50% of income
options.reflection: EMIs above 50% are extremely risky and can lead to financial distress.

options.label: Any percentage is fine as long as you pay on time
options.reflection: Percentage matters greatly for financial safety and ability to handle emergencies.

options.label: Less than 30% of income
options.reflection: Exactly! Keeping EMIs below 30% of income provides financial stability and flexibility.

stages.prompt: What happens when EMI exceeds 50% of your income?

options.label: Increases financial safety
options.reflection: Actually, high EMIs decrease financial safety by limiting your ability to handle other expenses.

options.label: Has no impact on financial safety
options.reflection: High EMIs significantly impact financial safety by consuming most of your income.

options.label: Reduces financial safety and flexibility
options.reflection: Yes! High EMIs leave little room for other expenses and emergencies.

options.label: Makes it easier to save money
options.reflection: High EMIs make it much harder to save money as most income goes to EMI payments.

stages.prompt: Why is it important to consider your income percentage when taking a loan?

options.label: Income percentage doesn't matter for loan eligibility
options.reflection: Income percentage is crucial for determining your ability to repay without financial stress.

options.label: To ensure you can meet EMI obligations without compromising other needs
options.reflection: Absolutely! This ensures financial stability and ability to handle other expenses.

options.label: Only the loan amount matters, not the EMI percentage
options.reflection: The EMI percentage relative to income is critical for determining affordability.

options.label: To maximize the loan amount you can take
options.reflection: The goal should be affordability, not maximizing loan amount beyond your capacity.

stages.prompt: What is the best approach to determine your EMI capacity?

options.label: Keep EMIs below 30% of your gross income
options.reflection: Perfect! This ensures financial safety and leaves room for other expenses and savings.

options.label: Take loans with EMIs up to 60% of income
options.reflection: EMIs at 60% are extremely risky and can lead to financial distress.

options.label: Match EMIs to your highest earning month
options.reflection: EMIs should be based on average income, not peak earnings to ensure consistency.

options.label: Focus only on the loan tenure, not EMI percentage
options.reflection: EMI percentage is crucial regardless of tenure to ensure affordability.
